author: codex-root

patch.value: Restore live Home Assistant API parity and agent workflows

Research current official Home Assistant REST and WebSocket contracts, validate this CLI against the local production instance, repair runtime/API drift, expand typed agent discovery helpers, and leave setup/output/help/docs fully production-ready.

Every REST command reaches the live API through the supported Undici contract; typed WebSocket target helpers match current Home Assistant response fields and expose triggers, conditions, and services; setup wizard and global settings validate against the local instance without persisting private data in the repository; all formats, help paths, unit/coverage/release gates, and live read-only E2E checks pass; reviewed PR is merged and verified on master
Official API docs, complete pm inventory, current code, and live failure evidence reviewed
A reliable agent-first CLI that works against current Home Assistant rather than only mocked tests
Restores all REST functionality and improves deterministic target discovery for automation agents
Current Home Assistant can be discovered and operated safely through typed and passthrough CLI surfaces
Undici 8 currently makes every REST command fail before network I/O

message: Create duplicate-safe live API parity feature from official and instance evidence

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Official Home Assistant REST and WebSocket docs were reviewed on 2026-07-21; the live instance will be the compatibility oracle for supported, integration-dependent surfaces.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Never persist the LAN URL, access token, username, password, entity names, location, or other private instance data in repository files or pm evidence. Record only redacted capability counts and contract-level findings.

author: codex-root

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Official Home Assistant REST and WebSocket documentation plus current core source were cross-checked against live 2026.4.4 behavior. The generic ws call remains the complete dynamic escape surface; stable typed helpers cover target extraction and automation primitive discovery.

message: Build the feature dependency graph from all delivery workstreams

value.author: codex-root
value.text: PR #60 opened at https://github.com/unbraind/homeassistant-cli/pull/60 from verified commit a07bdcb. The PR description links the complete PM lineage; Greptile, Gemini, and CodeRabbit reviews were requested for the exact head.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: PR #60 merged to master as 8c1c67e8178cdec1e0938744863fd3633bfddeeb after every required protected context passed and all bot feedback surfaces were inventoried, acknowledged, and left with zero actionable findings. Exact merge-head release:verify then passed locally: 1186 tests, 100/100/100/100 coverage, static/docstring/duplication/PM/security/history/commit gates, plus live read-only REST and WebSocket smoke through the installed hassio binary.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: For dynamic Home Assistant WebSocket coverage, a typed command for every integration is neither stable nor complete. The robust agent interface combines typed helpers for durable discovery contracts with a schema-validating generic passthrough for integration-defined command types.

patch.value: Current Home Assistant REST transport and typed target discovery were restored and expanded, agent/help/setup/output/docs/quality surfaces hardened, and PR #60 merged after complete hosted and live verification.
Current Home Assistant can be safely discovered and operated through an intuitive typed and passthrough CLI with complete production gates.

Live REST and WebSocket workflows pass through the installed hassio release; 1186 tests provide 100/100/100/100 coverage; strict docs, static, PM, security, release, format, help, and bot-review gates pass; PR #60 is merged.
All acceptance criteria met in merged PR #60 and exact-master/live proof; publishing remains intentionally manual.
